channel.title: Oldies TAL Feed
channel.description: Random episode 10+ years old, updated weekly

item.title: [Oldies] 436: The Psychopath Test
item.description:
https://www.thisamericanlife.org/436/the-psychopath-test

We heard about a test that could determine if someone was a psychopath. So, naturally, our staff decided to take it.

Prologue
Ira explains that when the radio staff decided to take a test that reveals who is a psychopath, very quickly everyone came to believe that the highest score would go to either Robyn, Jane, or him. (6 minutes) by Ira Glass

Act One
Alix Spiegel tells the story of Robert Dixon, who's in a maximum security prison in Vacaville California and is unlikely to ever get parole because of his score on the psychopath test. The test also is called "the checklist" or, more formally, the PCL-R, which stands for "Psychopathy Check List—Revised." Alix tells the story of its creation and reports that the man who created the test, Bob Hare, is concerned at how it's being used today in the criminal justice system. A version of this story aired on NPR's All Things Considered . (28 minutes) by Alix Spiegel

Act Two
Jon Ronson investigates whether corporate leaders can, in fact, be psychopaths by visiting a former Sunbeam CEO named Al Dunlap. This is an excerpt from Ronson's book, The Psychopath Test . (15 minutes) by Jon Ronson

Act Three
Ira and the radio show staff get their results on the psychopath test from Dr. David Bernstein, of Forensic Consultants, LLC. , who administered the test to them. (6 minutes) by Ira Glass

Originally Aired: 2011-05-27
